review_note: Legitimate apartment-price discussion is pushed to spam by eleven digits and three currency markers; this is a clear cue-induced false positive.
A ringtone-club solicitation has almost none of the usual numeric or contact cues so the shallow model misses clear spam that the text model detects.
A premium-rate adult-chat lure is recovered mainly from a telephone number and price marker while the full-text baseline remains below threshold.
Phone and reply-cost markers drive the shallow probability to nearly one even though the full-text baseline remains below threshold.
A mobile-club offer is missed by the shallow probe because it lacks the usual phone currency and promotional-token pattern.
A benign chain-message questionnaire contains many digits and a reply cue which causes a shallow false positive despite ordinary conversational context.
An informal message about a pay rise contains digits currency and uppercase text which pushes the shallow model over the spam threshold.
URL stop and price cues recover a genuine subscription spam message that the frozen full-text baseline narrowly misses.
The public spam label reads like an ordinary joke; a joint model miss does not isolate a shortcut and is better handled by label adjudication.
This appears to be a recipient reply rather than an originating promotion so the disagreement is likely label noise rather than shortcut evidence.
Recruitment advertising with a URL and contact request is policy-sensitive so calling the shallow prediction a false positive would overstate the evidence.
This message is an exact-text duplicate of H0949 and should not be counted as an independent shortcut example.
The free-SMS service message is both policy-sensitive and duplicated so it is retained for ambiguity review rather than shortcut ranking.
The quiz could be a paid service but lacks explicit price or contact evidence; two model misses do not identify a specific shortcut mechanism.
The generic horoscope-style wording has uncertain label semantics and no strong shallow-versus-text contrast.
The word call is polysemous but the full-text score is also near the boundary making this weaker than the retained benign hard negatives.
